concerns.text: The plan cites 'Materials Project MP-2020.12.1' but explicitly states 'NO verified source found in the provided # Verified datasets block' and relies on an 'assumed' Zenodo mirror. The research strategy admits the dataset URL is unverified and the fallback (matminer built-in) is uncertain. A plan cannot proceed with a primary dataset that is not confirmed to exist or be accessible via a verified source. This is a fatal data resource gap.

The plan assumes processing '~12,500 to 150,000' compounds. The lower bound (12.5k) suggests a heavily filtered subset, while the upper bound (150k) suggests the full MP-2020.12.1 release. Without a verified source and specific filtering criteria (e.g., 'only stable phases', 'only specific crystal systems'), the dataset size is speculative. The compute feasibility (6 hours on 2 CPU) depends entirely on the actual row count, which is currently unverified.

The 'Verified Accuracy' check claims compliance by stating 'All citations in research.md will be cross-referenced', but the research.md itself admits the primary dataset citation is missing/assumed. The plan cannot claim compliance with a verification principle when the underlying data source is unverified.
